name: Thai Chicken Salad
recipeIngredient: 2 large chicken breast halves (skinned, boned), cut into cubes
1 head romaine lettuce, torn into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shallow bowl, mix cilantro with 1/2 tablespoon tamari (soy sauce), the water, fish sauce, garlic, and pepper.
2. Add chicken cubes to the mixture and stir to coat evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 24 hours.
3. Soak cellophane noodles in enough water to cover them for 20 minutes. Drain and gently squeeze out excess moisture with a dish towel, cutting into 2-inch pieces.
4. Preheat the broiler. Remove chicken from the marinade (discard it) and place in a baking dish. Broil for 10 minutes or until cooked through.
5. While the chicken is cooking, make the dressing by combining lime juice, remaining 1/2 tablespoon tamari (soy sauce), and sugar in a screw-top jar. Shake well.
6. Arrange lettuce on plates. Place noodles on top of the lettuce and arrange chicken, tomatoes, and cucumber pieces over the noodles.
7. Drizzle the dressing over everything and sprinkle scallions and peanuts evenly. Serve 4.
Note: Look for cellophane noodles and Thai fish sauce in the ethnic foods aisle or an Asian market. Feel free to substitute shrimp, beef, or tofu for the chicken.
